simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea:
**Simplified Version:**

Basic Patient Information and Exam Details

simplified_details.simplified_detail: PATIENT: JOHN SMITH
Date of Birth: 5/5/1955
**Test:** A special scan that uses strong magnets and radio waves to create detailed pictures of your neck bones.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: Reason for this special imaging test
original_details: Left neck and shoulder pain for 2 months

simplified_details.original_detail: Left neck and shoulder pain for 2 months
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Left neck and shoulder pain for 2 months

simplified_main_ideas.original_main_idea: Overall Diagnosis: Multilevel Degenerative Disease and Facet Arthropathy
simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: Main Finding: Changes affecting many areas due to wear and tear, along with problems in the small joints of your backbones.
original_details: Most severe from C4-5 through C6-7

simplified_details.original_detail: Most severe from C4-5 through C6-7
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Most severe from the area between the 4th and 5th neck bones through the area between the 6th and 7th neck bones.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: The way your neck bones are lined up is not normal.
original_details: Straightening of the normal cervical lordosis
May be positional or related to muscular spasm

simplified_details.original_detail: Straightening of the normal cervical lordosis
simplified_details.simplified_detail: The natural inward curve of your neck has become straighter.

simplified_details.original_detail: May be positional or related to muscular spasm
simplified_details.simplified_detail: May be related to your body's position or to muscle cramps.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea:
Here is the simplified version of the medical text:

**General things found about the spongy cushions between your back bones and the age-related wear and tear changes in your spine.**
original_details: Moderate to severe disc space narrowing from C4-5 through C6-7

simplified_details.original_detail: Moderate to severe disc space narrowing from C4-5 through C6-7
simplified_details.simplified_detail: There is moderate to severe narrowing of the spaces between your neck bones, specifically between the 4th and 5th, the 5th and 6th, and the 6th and 7th neck bones.
Noticeable wear and tear changes in front of the backbone.

simplified_main_ideas.original_main_idea: Spinal Cord and Cerebellar Tonsil Status
simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea:
Here is the simplified version of your medical text:

The condition of the main nerve bundle running down your back and the lowest part of the brain that controls balance and movement.
original_details: Spinal cord is normal in caliber and signal intensity

simplified_details.original_detail: Spinal cord is normal in caliber and signal intensity
simplified_details.simplified_detail: The main nerve cord in your back is normal in its size and how it shows up on the pictures.
The lowest parts of the brain that control balance and movement are found where they should be.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: What was seen in the spongy material inside your bones
original_details: Low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow surrounding the left C4-5 facet joint

simplified_details.simplified_detail: No abnormal areas on the bones that are causing damage.

simplified_details.original_detail: Low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow surrounding the left C4-5 facet joint
simplified_details.simplified_detail: A darker appearance on the MRI within the spongy material inside the bone around the small joint on the left side between the fourth and fifth neck bones.
Likely caused by wear and tear in the small joints in your back.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: What was found at the third and fourth neck bones.
original_details: Deformity of the ventral thecal sac

simplified_details.simplified_detail: In one specific spot, the gel-like cushion between your bones is pushing out in the middle by 1 to 2 millimeters.
Mild extra bone growth in the small joints on the right side of the neck bones.

simplified_details.original_detail: Deformity of the ventral thecal sac
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Abnormal shape of the front part of the sac that holds the spinal cord and its fluid.
Mild narrowing of the space around the spinal cord
There is no important narrowing of the openings where the nerves pass through.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: Findings at the area between the 4th and 5th neck bones.
original_details: Diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the left
Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
Moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing

simplified_details.original_detail: Diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the left
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Widespread bone spurs growing from the cushions between your backbones, with these spurs being more noticeable on the left side.
A problem with the small joint on the left side in the back of the spine.

simplified_details.original_detail: Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
simplified_details.simplified_detail: There is some flattening of the front part of the protective sac around your spinal cord.
Mild narrowing of the space around the spinal cord.
Moderate narrowing of the right nerve opening.

simplified_details.original_detail: Moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Moderate to severe narrowing of the openings on the left side where the nerves come out.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: Findings at the area between the 5th and 6th bones in your neck.
original_details: Diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the right
Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
Moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing

simplified_details.original_detail: Diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the right
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Widespread extra bone growths on the soft cushions between your back bones, which are more noticeable on the right side.
There is mild thickening or slight enlargement of the small joints that connect the bones in your spine, on both sides.

simplified_details.original_detail: Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
simplified_details.simplified_detail: The front part of the protective sac that holds your spinal cord and nerves is flattened.
Mild narrowing of the space around the spinal cord.

simplified_details.original_detail: Moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Moderate to severe narrowing of the openings where the nerves come out of the spine on the right side.
Mild narrowing of the nerve opening on the left side.

simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea:
Here is the simplified version of the medical text:

What was found in the area between the 6th and 7th bones of the neck.
original_details: Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
Moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing

simplified_details.simplified_detail: Widespread bone spurs growing from the cushions between your bones.

simplified_details.original_detail: Flattening of the ventral thecal sac
simplified_details.simplified_detail: The front part of the protective sac around the spinal cord and fluid is flattened.
Mild narrowing of the space around the spinal cord.

simplified_details.original_detail: Moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Moderate to severe narrowing of the openings where the nerves come out on both sides.

simplified_main_ideas.original_main_idea: Absence of Significant Findings at C2-3 and C7-T1
simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: No important problems were found between the second and third bones in your neck, and no important problems were found between the seventh bone in your neck and the first bone in your upper back.
original_details: C2-3: No disc osteophyte complex, spinal canal stenosis, or neural foraminal narrowing
C7-T1: No disc osteophyte complex, spinal canal stenosis, or neural foraminal narrowing

simplified_details.original_detail: C2-3: No disc osteophyte complex, spinal canal stenosis, or neural foraminal narrowing
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Between the second and third bones in your neck: There is no bulging disc combined with extra bone growths, no narrowing of the space around your spinal cord, and no narrowing of the openings where your nerves exit.

simplified_details.original_detail: C7-T1: No disc osteophyte complex, spinal canal stenosis, or neural foraminal narrowing
simplified_details.simplified_detail: Between the 7th neck bone and the 1st upper back bone: No problems involving the cushion between the bones and extra bone growths, no narrowing of the space around the spinal cord, or narrowing of the openings where the nerves exit the spine.

simplified_main_ideas.original_main_idea: Absence of Paraspinal Soft Tissue Mass
simplified_main_ideas.simplified_main_idea: There is no lump or growth in the muscles, fat, and other soft parts next to the backbone (spine).

simplified_details.simplified_detail: No lump or growth in the muscles and fat next to your spine.
